title: Verizon (VZ)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Verizon using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what VZ is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
">
Verizon is a mature telecom giant in a saturated market. Revenue growth is typically in the low single digits. Free cash flow is relatively stable, though high capital intensity limits explosive growth. A 2% growth rate reflects modest price increases and slow subscriber additions.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
">
Verizon is a mature telecom giant in a saturated market. Revenue growth is typically in the low single digits. Free cash flow is relatively stable, though high capital intensity limits explosive growth. A 2% growth rate reflects modest price increases and slow subscriber additions.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
">
Verizon is a mature telecom giant in a saturated market. Revenue growth is typically in the low single digits. Free cash flow is relatively stable, though high capital intensity limits explosive growth. A 2% growth rate reflects modest price increases and slow subscriber additions.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a dividend discount model (DDM) for a telecom?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Verizon (VZ) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
